subject: Add Network Logging Feature
commit_message:
Add Network Logging Feature

This is part 1 of introducing the mdc filter feature. Network
logging has to have interception points in order to pre/post
process messages coming in from a network endpoint.

The OrderedService interface and OrderedServiceImpl of the
drools-pdp project have now been migrated to common but is
also left in drools-pdp to mitigate disruption to existing
features. However for features that need to use common's
OrderedServiceImpl and drools-pdp version of OrderedServiceImpl
there will be a conflict with getSequenceNumber(). So a migration
of the other features is suggested.

Network logging is moved to its own util class so that feature
hooks can be invoked any time an event is being logged by a
network logger. This util class will also be accessible to
drools-applications in the case where drools-applications
would like to invoke network logging features for REST events.

Change-Id: I83d7c46e5abb351486f841c3be4d9009f7992476
Issue-ID: POLICY-1499
Signed-off-by: Daniel Cruz <dc443y@att.com>

Patch Set 1:

I'm not too familiar with the logging standards after Amsterdam so I will have to get back to you on that. However the logger that this code uses is the standard network log that has been in use since Amsterdam so if the standard network logger is compliant than it should be fine.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

A few logistics things that need to be taken care of: 

a) add junits, 

b) a plan to have coordinated changes submitted too as drools-pdp and drools-applications. If you can provide feedback.
  
As a side note, I think this class should be deleted or consolidated with NetLoggerUtil.java:

https://git.onap.org/policy/common/tree/utils/src/main/java/org/onap/policy/common/utils/slf4j/LoggerFactoryWrapper.java as it does not have relevance.

Question:

Does this cover all sources, specifically Noop sources?

Patch Set 1:

> A few logistics things that need to be taken care of:
 > 
 > a) add junits,
 > 
 > b) a plan to have coordinated changes submitted too as drools-pdp
 > and drools-applications. If you can provide feedback.
 > 
 > As a side note, I think this class should be deleted or
 > consolidated with NetLoggerUtil.java:
 > 
 > https://git.onap.org/policy/common/tree/utils/src/main/java/org/onap/policy/common/utils/slf4j/LoggerFactoryWrapper.java
 > as it does not have relevance.
 > 
 > Question:
 > 
 > Does this cover all sources, specifically Noop sources?

a) Sure, will add in the next review
b) Part 1 is to have this submitted as it won't disrupt any current features in the platform. Part 2 would be to submit two pull requests simultaneously in drools-pdp and drools-applications to migrate all features to use common's OrderedService. Once those are merged part 3 is to add the mdc filter feature. I understand these changes are quite disruptive and am open to suggestions for another approach. I found the trade offs the most fitting for what the feature needs, which is a way to intercept messages before they are logged so they can be placed in the MDC to be used by logback. Ideally the least disruptive change would have this be done at the controller level but that would introduce duplicate messages if 2 controllers ingest a message from a shared topic.

On the LoggerFactoryWrapper, I agree and will proceed with the changes.

On your question, when I was looking through the new topic code it appeared that both NoopTopicSource and NoopTopicSink call the NoopTopicEndpoint's io() method, which would mean that noop sources would be covered. Just an fyi, one thing that seemed like a minor bug to me is that by calling io() for both the noop source/sink, the method always logs the message as an "OUT" message rather than an "IN" message if it is a source topic. But perhaps I am missing some context.

Patch Set 1:

Thanks Daniel, plan you proposed for changes is good with me.
Can you also take care of the io() potential issue you mentioned, since it is related to this work?
Usually, each review comes together with junits, I'll let the other commiters decide if they are ok with submitting junits in a different review.
